note: All quantitative data verified against source. Anthropic Economic Index data from published reports (Feb 2025, Sep 2025, Jan 2026). BLS data from public API (LAUS county-level, aggregated to Weldon Cooper regions). BuildFirst observations are qualitative.

regional_unemployment.title: Unemployment Rate by Virginia Region
regional_unemployment.subtitle: BLS Local Area Unemployment Statistics, county-level aggregated to Weldon Cooper Center regions
regional_unemployment.source: Bureau of Labor Statistics, LAUS program
regional_unemployment.scenario_reading: The persistent 1.5-2.5 percentage point gap between NoVA/Richmond and Southside/Southwest is consistent with the 'Pockets of Excellence' scenario. If this gap were narrowing, it would signal 'Build It Yourself.' If widening sharply, 'The Widening Gap.'

anthropic_augmentation.title: AI Usage: Automation vs. Augmentation
anthropic_augmentation.subtitle: Anthropic Economic Index, share of Claude conversations by interaction mode (US national)
anthropic_augmentation.source: Anthropic Economic Index (Hugging Face, CC-BY). Reports: Feb 2025, Sep 2025, Jan 2026.
anthropic_augmentation.scenario_reading: Augmentation dominance signals people are thinking with AI (Build It Yourself). Automation dominance signals vendor-driven, passive use (Software Does It For You). The Aug 2025 swing to automation was the first time it overtook augmentation. It swung back by Nov 2025. The trend is volatile but augmentation-leaning.

anthropic_geographic_gini.title: Geographic Concentration of AI Usage (US)
anthropic_geographic_gini.subtitle: Gini coefficient across US states. Lower = more evenly distributed. 0 = perfect equality.
anthropic_geographic_gini.scenario_reading: Falling Gini = AI usage spreading geographically. At the Aug-Nov rate, state-level parity in 2-5 years. This favors 'Build It Yourself' (distributed) over 'Pockets of Excellence' (concentrated). If Gini reversed and started rising, that would signal 'The Widening Gap.'

anthropic_task_coverage.title: AI Task Coverage by Occupation Category
anthropic_task_coverage.subtitle: Share of occupational tasks with observed AI usage vs. theoretical capability
anthropic_task_coverage.source: Anthropic Economic Index + Eloundou et al. (2023) theoretical scores
anthropic_task_coverage.scenario_reading: The gap between theoretical capability and observed usage is enormous. Computer & Math uses 33% of what is feasible. Most occupations use under 15%. This gap IS the fluency question. In 'Build It Yourself,' the observed bars grow across all categories. In 'Software Does It For You,' they grow only in categories where vendors embed AI. In 'Pockets of Excellence,' they grow only in metro-concentrated knowledge-worker categories.

scenario_strength.title: Current Scenario Signal Strength
scenario_strength.subtitle: Based on all indicators assessed against six data sources
scenario_strength.reading: Virginia is currently tracking between 'Software Does It For You' and 'Pockets of Excellence.' Broad shallow adoption is measurably happening (SaaS AI embedding, adoption-without-fluency gap). Metro pockets of deeper engagement are forming. 'Build It Yourself' remains aspirational. 'The Widening Gap' is a risk if the economy softens.
